methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $125M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
Freeform is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $125M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable set of 9 private companies and 1 public entity operating in the same sector at equivalent or proximate stages. The private peer group includes 1X Technologies, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Collaborative Robotics, and Fairmat, spanning valuations from $300M to $10.0B.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method, using total capital raised as a proxy base ($125M), applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $238M. The peer-median method calculates the median valuation across six same-sector, same-stage comparables at $1.1B, with a range of $300M to $10.0B. These methods produce materially different results, reflecting wide dispersion in peer outcomes—likely attributable to heterogeneous technical maturity, market adoption, and capital efficiency within the robotics sector.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $202M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $656M. The gap between stage-progression and peer-median reflects meaningful uncertainty around Freeform's positioning relative to the peer set. Key sensitivities include: relative progress on commercialization, capital deployment efficiency versus peers, and sector-level maturation risk. Additional clarity on revenue traction and near-term deployment milestones would narrow this range.
